items.title: Hear Bright Eyes & The Lumineers’ Tracks From A New Felice Brothers Tribute Album
items.summary: New York folk-rock ramblers the Felice Brothers started out busking in subway stations more than two decades ago, and now they're getting their own tribute album.

items.title: The Olivia Tremor Control Announce First New Album In 27 Years The Same Place
items.summary: The idea of a new Olivia Tremor Control was basically unthinkable, but it's happening anyway. The lo-fi psychedelic pop band was a key driving force in Athens, Georgia's Elephant 6 collective in the '90s, but they only released two cult-beloved albums, 1996's Dusk At Cubist Castle and 1999's Black Foliage: Animation Music Volume One, before breaking up.

items.title: Generacion Suicida Are Here To Depress The Punks
items.summary: "We're here to depress the punks, I guess." That was Tony Abarca, frontman of the LA band Generacion Suicida, talking to a restaurant patio full of punks in Richmond on Wednesday night. During one of his infrequent moments of between-songs patter, Abarca admitted to being "not much of a talker," but "depress the punks, I guess" is a pretty accurate summary of Generacion Suicida's mission. They're definitely a punk band.

items.title: MEMORIALS touring ahead of Hopscotch Fest in September
items.summary: MEMORIALS, the UK duo of Verity Susman (Electrelane) and Matthew Simms (Wire, It Hugs Back), released their terrific second album ‘All Clouds Bring Not Rain’ this year.

items.title: Deep Cut Friday: “King of the World” by Steely Dan
items.summary: Each week, SPIN digs into the catalogs of great artists and highlights songs you might not know for our Deep Cut Friday series. Steely Dan thrived in the age of the greatest hits compilation, and the band’s 1978 Greatest Hits collected most of the band’s popular singles on two LPs. In fact, it was so […]
